Index.title: Free Online Tools for Filipinos
Index.subtitle: Accurate calculators, generators, and utilities designed specifically for the Philippines.

Search.title: Search Tools
Search.enterSearch: Enter a search query to find tools.
Search.noResults: No results found. Try different keywords.

SalaryCalculator.grossSalaryHint: Your basic salary before any deductions.
SalaryCalculator.estimateDisclaimer: * This is an estimate based on standard 2026 rates.
SalaryCalculator.seoTitle: How to Compute Your Net Salary in the Philippines
SalaryCalculator.seoDesc: Calculating your take-home pay involves deducting your mandatory government contributions (SSS, PhilHealth, Pag-IBIG) and withholding tax from your gross basic salary.
SalaryCalculator.seoGovtSSS: SSS: The employee share is 5% of the Monthly Salary Credit (MSC). The maximum MSC is ₱35,000, meaning the maximum employee deduction is ₱1,750.
SalaryCalculator.seoGovtPhilHealth: PhilHealth: The premium rate is 5% of your basic salary, split equally between you and your employer (2.5% each). The salary floor is ₱10,000 and the ceiling is ₱100,000.
SalaryCalculator.seoGovtPagibig: Pag-IBIG: The standard employee contribution is ₱200 per month (2% of the ₱10,000 maximum fund salary).
SalaryCalculator.seoTaxDesc: To find your taxable income, subtract your total contributions from your gross salary. Then, apply the TRAIN Law tax brackets to compute your withholding tax. If your taxable income is below ₱20,833 per month (₱250,000 annually), you are exempt from income tax.

ThirteenthMonth.title: 13th Month Pay Calculator
ThirteenthMonth.subtitle: Calculate your prorated 13th month pay and check if it exceeds the ₱90k tax exemption limit.
ThirteenthMonth.basicSalaryHint: Exclude overtime, holiday pay, and allowances.
ThirteenthMonth.howComputedDesc: By Philippine Law (P.D. 851), your 13th month pay is strictly 1/12th of the total basic salary you earned within a calendar year.
ThirteenthMonth.taxabilityDesc: Under the TRAIN Law, the 13th month pay and other equivalent bonuses are tax-exempt up to ₱90,000. Any amount exceeding this limit is added to your taxable income for the year.

BudgetCalculator.title: Budget & Reverse Salary Calculator (2026)
BudgetCalculator.subtitle: List your monthly expenses and we'll tell you exactly what gross salary you should ask for.
BudgetCalculator.targetNetPayHint: How much cash you need in your bank account every month.
BudgetCalculator.extraSavingsHint: Any extra cash you want on top of your fixed expenses.
BudgetCalculator.minimumSalaryTip: This is the minimum gross salary you should negotiate.
BudgetCalculator.howItWorksDesc: This tool works backwards. It adds up all your required expenses to find your target net pay, then reverse-engineers the SSS, PhilHealth, Pag-IBIG, and Withholding Tax deductions to find the exact gross salary you should ask for in your job interview.
BudgetCalculator.disclaimer: * Based on SSS, PhilHealth, Pag-IBIG rates and TRAIN Law tax brackets. Private sector employee assumed.

CarLoanCalculator.title: Car Loan & Amortization Calculator
CarLoanCalculator.subtitle: Compute your monthly payment, total interest, and see why Bank Financing beats Dealer In-House Financing.
CarLoanCalculator.vehiclePriceHint: The cash price of the car you are buying.
CarLoanCalculator.interestRateHint: Typical bank rates are 8-10%, while in-house is 15-20%!
CarLoanCalculator.bankSavingsDesc: By getting a car loan from a commercial bank (at {interestRate}%) instead of dealer in-house financing (usually ~{inHouseRate}%), you will save:
CarLoanCalculator.bankVsInhouseDesc: Bank financing is almost ALWAYS cheaper. Dealerships often lure you in with 'low down payment' promos, but compensate by charging extremely high interest rates (15%+). Always try to get pre-approved by a bank first!

HomeLoanCalculator.title: Home Loan & Amortization Calculator
HomeLoanCalculator.subtitle: Estimate your monthly mortgage payments, down payment options, and total interest charges for commercial bank home loans.
HomeLoanCalculator.propertyPriceHint: The market value or selling price of the home.
HomeLoanCalculator.interestRateHint: Bank home loan rates typically range from 6% to 8% fixed.
HomeLoanCalculator.refinancingDesc: Banks offer fixed terms (e.g., 1-year, 5-year fixed). After the fixed period, the interest rate will repriced based on current market rates. Keep this in mind when budgeting for long terms!

BackpayCalculator.title: Final Pay & Backpay Calculator
BackpayCalculator.subtitle: Estimate your prorated 13th month, leave conversions, and final pay after resignation in the Philippines.
BackpayCalculator.daysWorkedHint: Days worked in your final cut-off that haven't been paid
BackpayCalculator.monthsWorkedHint: Number of months worked from Jan 1 up to your last day
BackpayCalculator.unusedLeavesHint: Total VLs/SLs convertible to cash (check company policy)
BackpayCalculator.totalDeductionsHint: Unliquidated cash advances, remaining company loans, final tax, etc.

TaxOptimizer.title: 8% vs Graduated Tax Optimizer
TaxOptimizer.subtitle: Find out which BIR tax classification saves you the most money: 8% Flat Rate, OSD, or Itemized Deductions.
TaxOptimizer.grossIncomeHint: Your total sales or receipts for the entire year before any expenses.
TaxOptimizer.annualExpensesHint: Your actual business expenses with official receipts (rent, utilities, supplies).
TaxOptimizer.warningVat: Since your income exceeds ₱3,000,000, you must register as a VAT taxpayer. The 8% flat rate is no longer applicable.
TaxOptimizer.saveAmount: You save {amount} compared to the most expensive option!

SSSPension.title: SSS Retirement Pension Calculator
SSSPension.subtitle: Estimate your monthly lifetime pension based on your SSS contributions and credited years of service.
SSSPension.amscHint: Your average monthly salary credit for the last 60 months before retirement.
SSSPension.cysHint: Total years you've contributed to SSS (minimum 10 years).
SSSPension.warningMinimum: You need at least 10 years of service (120 contributions) to qualify for a monthly pension. Otherwise, you will only receive a lump-sum amount.
SSSPension.totalAt80Hint: If you retire at 60 and live until 80, this is your total estimated cash received.

DigitalBank.title: Digital Bank Interest Calculator
DigitalBank.subtitle: Compare high-yield savings accounts across 10 top digital banks in the Philippines. Factors in the 20% withholding tax.
DigitalBank.monthlyAdditionHint: Amount you plan to add every month
DigitalBank.interestRateHint: You can manually adjust this if you have a promo rate.
DigitalBank.note: Note: Real-world results may vary slightly due to daily vs monthly compounding exact days, but this provides a highly accurate estimate. Philippine banks apply a 20% final withholding tax on interest earned.

PagibigMP2.title: Pag-IBIG MP2 Dividend Calculator
PagibigMP2.subtitle: Calculate your tax-free earnings from the Pag-IBIG Modified Pag-IBIG II (MP2) Savings Program over the 5-year lock-in period.
PagibigMP2.monthlyContributionHint: Must be at least ₱500 per month
PagibigMP2.dividendRateHint: Historical rates average between 6.0% to 7.5% annually.
PagibigMP2.note: Note: Dividends are calculated based on your Average Monthly Balance (AMB). This assumes contributions are made early in the month. Pag-IBIG MP2 earnings are 100% tax-free and government-guaranteed.

OvertimePay.title: Overtime & Holiday Pay Calculator
OvertimePay.subtitle: Calculate exactly how much you should be paid when working overtime, on rest days, or during Philippine holidays according to DOLE standards.
OvertimePay.specialHolidayRestDay: Special Non-Working Holiday AND Rest Day
OvertimePay.note: Note: This strictly follows the DOLE Handbook on Workers' Statutory Monetary Benefits. Regular Holidays worked are paid at 200%. Overtime on holidays has compounded premium multipliers.

SeparationPay.title: Separation Pay Calculator
SeparationPay.subtitle: Calculate your mandatory DOLE separation pay if you were terminated due to redundancy, retrenchment, disease, or business closure.
SeparationPay.reasonLaborSaving: Installation of labor-saving devices (1 month pay per year)
SeparationPay.reasonImpossible: Impossible reinstatement (1 month pay per year)
SeparationPay.reasonRetrenchment: Retrenchment to prevent losses (1/2 month pay per year)
SeparationPay.reasonClosure: Closure or cessation of business (1/2 month pay per year)
SeparationPay.reasonDisease: Disease / Prejudicial to health (1/2 month pay per year)
SeparationPay.yearsOfServiceHint: Note: A fraction of at least 6 months is considered as 1 whole year.
SeparationPay.note: Note: Minimum separation pay is always equivalent to at least one (1) month pay. This strictly follows Article 298 & 299 of the Labor Code of the Philippines.

SSSMaternity.title: SSS Maternity Benefit Calculator
SSSMaternity.subtitle: Calculate your cash benefit for normal delivery, cesarean section, solo parent, or miscarriage under the 105-Day Expanded Maternity Leave Law.
SSSMaternity.monthlySalaryHint: Your average monthly salary for the past year (capped at ₱30,000 max MSC).
SSSMaternity.note: Note: You must have paid at least 3 monthly contributions in the 12-month period immediately preceding the semester of your delivery or miscarriage to qualify.

FreelanceRate.title: Freelance Hourly Rate Calculator
FreelanceRate.subtitle: Calculate the exact hourly rate you should charge as a freelancer to hit your target net income, covering taxes, expenses, and vacation days.
FreelanceRate.targetNetHint: The take-home money you want in your bank account every month after taxes and expenses.
FreelanceRate.monthlyExpensesHint: Internet, software subscriptions, coworking space, electricity.
FreelanceRate.billableHoursHint: Actual hours you can charge clients for (excluding admin work). Default is 30.
FreelanceRate.vacationDaysHint: Number of unpaid days off you plan to take.
FreelanceRate.exchangeRateNote: Based on an estimated exchange rate of $1 = ₱56.
FreelanceRate.note: Note: This calculation automatically reverse-engineers the BIR 8% flat rate tax (if exceeding ₱250,000 gross) so you are guaranteed your target net income.

PagibigAffordability.title: Pag-IBIG Housing Loan Affordability Calculator
PagibigAffordability.subtitle: Find out exactly how much housing loan you can afford based on your salary, age, and Pag-IBIG's 35% capacity-to-pay rule.
PagibigAffordability.fixingPeriodHint: Shorter fixing periods have lower rates, but they get re-priced sooner.
PagibigAffordability.maxTermHint: Pag-IBIG allows up to 30 years, but you must pay it off before age 70.
PagibigAffordability.cappedHint: Capped at Pag-IBIG's maximum loan limit of ₱6,000,000.
PagibigAffordability.note: Note: Pag-IBIG requires that your monthly amortization does not exceed 35% of your gross monthly income (or 30% for loans above ₱1.25M, though this calculator uses a strict 35% baseline for standard processing).

ToolFooter.readGuideDesc: Want to know the exact formula behind this calculator? Check out our comprehensive, human-written guide that explains the law, computations, and exact rules.

GSISPension.title: GSIS Pension Calculator
GSISPension.subtitle: Calculate your GSIS Basic Monthly Pension (BMP) based on RA 8291.
GSISPension.amcDesc: Your average salary for the last 36 months of service.
GSISPension.cvsDesc: Must be at least 15 years to qualify for a regular pension.
GSISPension.minServiceWarn: * Must have at least 15 years of service.

DFAAge.title: Exact Age Calculator (Philippines)
DFAAge.subtitle: Calculate your exact age in years, months, and days for DFA Passports, PRC Exams, and Civil Service applications.
DFAAge.targetDateHint: Defaults to today. Useful for checking age on a future exam or travel date.
DFAAge.statusMinorDesc: Requires DSWD clearance for solo travel and parental presence for DFA passport application.
DFAAge.statusAdultDesc: Eligible to sign legal contracts and travel independently.
DFAAge.statusPRCDesc: Many professional boards require you to be exactly 21 years old on the day of the exam.
DFAAge.statusSeniorDesc: Eligible for 20% discount and VAT exemption under RA 9994.

BillSplitter.title: GCash & Maya Bill Splitter
BillSplitter.subtitle: Easily split restaurant bills for your group. Includes options for tips and calculates the exact GCash/Maya transfer amount per person.
BillSplitter.sendViaDesc: Ask each person to send this exact amount to the payer.

BIRWithholdingTax.title: Expanded Withholding Tax (EWT) Calculator
BIRWithholdingTax.subtitle: Calculate BIR Form 2307 deductions for freelancers, professionals, and suppliers (1%, 2%, 5%, 10%).
BIRWithholdingTax.vatInclusiveLabel: Is the Gross Amount VAT Inclusive (12%)?
BIRWithholdingTax.vatHint: If VAT inclusive, the withholding tax is calculated strictly on the Vatable Sales base (Gross ÷ 1.12), not the total invoice amount.
BIRWithholdingTax.wtHint: Ask your client for BIR Form 2307 for this amount.

MotorcycleLoan.title: Motorcycle Loan Calculator (Hulugan)
MotorcycleLoan.subtitle: Calculate your exact monthly amortization, total interest, and hidden costs for in-house dealer financing (Motortrade, Wheeltek, etc.).
MotorcycleLoan.interestRateHint: PH dealers usually charge 1.5% to 2.5% flat interest per month. This is NOT annual compounding interest.
MotorcycleLoan.warningDesc: In-house dealer financing applies a flat monthly rate which results in an massive Effective Interest Rate (EIR). Consider saving up for a larger downpayment or using a bank personal loan instead.

PRCBoardExamRating.title: PRC Board Exam Rating Calculator
PRCBoardExamRating.subtitle: Calculate your General Weighted Average for NLE, CE, MTLE, and CPALE. Know exactly what scores you need to pass.

LatinHonors.title: Latin Honors Calculator (UP, PUP, UST, Ateneo, DLSU)
LatinHonors.subtitle: Check if your GWA or QPI qualifies for Cum Laude, Magna Cum Laude, or Summa Cum Laude based on your school's specific cutoffs.
LatinHonors.readGuideBtn: Read How to Get Latin Honors →

DOSTScholarshipStipend.title: DOST-SEI Scholarship Stipend Calculator
DOSTScholarshipStipend.subtitle: Calculate your exact yearly DOST scholarship benefits: living allowance, book allowance, tuition subsidy, and thesis grant.
DOSTScholarshipStipend.schoolPrivateNoTuition: Private HEI — Has Separate Tuition Scholarship
DOSTScholarshipStipend.tuitionHint: DOST will pay up to ₱40,000/sem for private HEIs.

QPIGPA.title: QPI / GPA Calculator (Ateneo, DLSU & 4.0 Scale)
QPIGPA.subtitle: Calculate your Quality Point Index or Cumulative GPA using the 4.0 grading scale. Add your courses and units to get your weighted average.

GrossFromTax.title: Reverse Tax to Gross Salary Calculator
GrossFromTax.subtitle: Enter the amount of withholding tax to estimate the equivalent gross monthly salary.

CHEDScholarship.title: CHED Scholarship Benefits Calculator
CHEDScholarship.subtitle: Calculate your exact annual benefits for CHED Merit (CMSP) and Tulong Dunong Program (TDP).
